feedback.text: I'd like to see one additional issue adressed by this intiative: access to software being sold under a perpetual license being revoked on the whims of the company operating the software. This is particularly present in the gaming industry, with games being made completely inaccessible to customers when the company decides it's no longer profitable for them to operate the game. Companies should be required to develop software with an end-of-life plan, so that when they no longer wish to actively support a piece of software, it is left in a functional state for the customers who purchased it.
